evals.prompt: Can you do a full SEO audit on https://www.allbirds.com/products/mens-tree-runners? I want to know why this page isn't ranking as well as it should.
evals.expected_output: Full structured audit detecting PDP page type, checking crawlability, title tag, meta description, schema via Rich Results Test, Core Web Vitals via Lighthouse, and providing prioritized recommendations with PDP-specific checks.
expectations: Correctly identifies the page type as a Product Detail Page (PDP)
Checks and reports on the title tag with actual content and character count
Checks and reports on meta description with actual content
Checks H1 heading and heading hierarchy
Mentions checking Rich Results Test for schema, not just raw HTML
Checks robots.txt and sitemap
Reports Core Web Vitals with actual scores (not just a link to PSI)
Provides a prioritized list of issues (Critical / High / Medium / Quick Wins)
Includes PDP-specific checks (product schema, reviews, unique description)

evals.expected_output: Full audit detecting Blog Post page type, checking Article schema via Rich Results Test, author E-E-A-T including helpful content signals, internal linking, content freshness, CWV, and blog-specific recommendations.
expectations: Correctly identifies the page type as a Blog Post or Article
Checks for Article or BlogPosting schema via Rich Results Test
Assesses author credentials and E-E-A-T signals
References people-first content signals or Who/How/Why framework from Google helpful content guidelines
Reports on content freshness (last updated date)
Checks title tag length and keyword placement
Reports Core Web Vitals with actual scores or explains why scores unavailable
Provides blog-specific recommendations

evals.prompt: I run a local plumbing business. Can you check the SEO on this homepage? https://www.joethegoatfarmer.com/
evals.expected_output: Full audit detecting Local Business page type, checking LocalBusiness schema, NAP visibility, location keywords, Google Map embed, robots.txt, sitemap, Core Web Vitals, and local SEO recommendations.
expectations: Detects the page type as Local Business or Homepage
Checks for LocalBusiness schema via Rich Results Test
Checks NAP (name, address, phone) visibility on the page
Checks whether location keywords appear in the title tag and H1
Checks robots.txt and sitemap
Reports Core Web Vitals
Provides local SEO specific recommendations

evals.prompt: Run a full SEO audit on https://www.nutrabay.com/ — it's a React SPA, so make sure you handle JS rendering properly.
evals.expected_output: Full audit that detects JS rendering, uses curl or puppeteer fallback to extract rendered DOM content, correctly identifies page type, checks schema, CWV via Lighthouse, and flags any JS-rendering SEO risks.
expectations: Detects or flags that the page uses JavaScript rendering (SPA/CSR)
Uses curl, mcp__puppeteer, or another fallback when WebFetch returns insufficient content
Successfully extracts page title, H1, or meta description from the rendered page
Reports page type based on rendered content
Checks schema markup (via Rich Results Test or puppeteer DOM extraction)
Reports Core Web Vitals
Flags any JS-rendering SEO risks (slow indexation, content not in initial HTML)
Provides prioritized recommendations

evals.prompt: just check the title tag and meta description on https://www.allbirds.com/products/mens-tree-runners — that's all I need
evals.expected_output: Focused response covering only title tag and meta description — not a full audit. Should quote exact content, character counts, and specific recommendations for each. No robots.txt, no CWV, no schema sections.
expectations: Recognizes this as a quick/focused check, not a full audit
Does NOT produce a full 7-phase audit report
Quotes the exact title tag with character count
Quotes the exact meta description (or notes it is missing) with character count
Provides specific improvement recommendations for title and meta description
Response is concise and focused — no crawlability, CWV, or schema sections
